target: 2. Town Hall Request
But I was really surprised...
What?
To think that you, Shin-chan, were acquaintances with *that* Kaori Koyuba-chan.
You heard we were classmates, right, Mio...
By the way, what do you mean by "*that*"? Is Koyuba-san really that famous?
Famous doesn't even begin to describe it. She's super famous!
An idol group? The center of it? Wasn't she doing something like that?
Why the question marks?
Well, I'm not exactly an expert on the subject either.
But if even someone like me who's out of touch with idols knows about her, I'm sure she's an amazing idol!
(That Koyuba-san is an amazing idol, huh...)
(It just doesn't click for me.)
So, anyway, Koyuba-san.
Y-yes!
What kind of relationship did you have with Shin-chan?
W-We weren't close enough for me to answer a question like that.
Is that so?
We only talked a little bit in the library... I think.
Yeah, that's right. I was on the library committee, so we just talked a little bit because of that.
Shin-chan has been a bookworm since way back when, after all.
What about you, Mio? Do you not remember Koyuba-san?
Ahaha... not really. Sorry about that.
It can't be helped. We were in different grades, too.
(Honestly, it's beyond surprising. To think that she, who gave off such a quiet impression in the past, would actually become an idol...)
Ah, by the way… Kofune-san.
What is it?
I was wondering if you could call me Kaori.
Is that okay! Then... Kaori-chan!
Isn't it rude to add "-chan" to someone older than you?
It's fine. It makes it easier for me to talk to you, too.
Ah, could you call me by my first name, Mio, as well!
Okay, then I'll call you Mio-chan.
Are you going to keep calling her "Koyuba-san", Shin-chan?
Yeah, it's fine to just keep it the same as always, right?
Y-Yeah, being called by my first name by Ajiro-kun is...
She says she doesn't want you to, Kaori-chan.
Th-That's not it! Being called by my first name by Ajiro-kun is... e-embarrassing.
Then I'll stick with Koyuba-san, same as always.
Y-Yeah...
Hey, my apologies. Sorry to keep you waiting.
Huh? Where's the guy from the Chamber of Commerce?
He went back because he has festival preparations to do. I'll be explaining things from here on out, so could you come over here?
Y-Yes, please!
I hear you two are going to help out as well. That's a real lifesaver.
Um, why exactly were we called here?
What, haven't you heard anything?
Yes…
Sorry, you two. I didn't explain it in detail, did I.
Actually, I came back home for this island's summer festival.
The summer festival... at Hitogashima Shrine?
Exactly, and we asked Koyuba-san to be the one to liven up that summer festival.
I'll do my best!
Even though she's a popular star and super busy, she squeezed us into her schedule and came all the way here.
Wow~. Coming all the way back just for the festival...
Of course, Koyuba-san truly loves this island from the bottom of her heart. She doesn't even miss a hometown tax donation.
This is my birthplace, after all... I've always thought that I'd like to return someday when I had the time!
Also, there was someone I've been wanting to see for a long time...
Someone you want to see? A friend or something?
Eh? Ah, a friend...? I-I wonder if that's it? Maybe not a friend, but...
I don't really get it, but it'd be great if you could reunite at this festival.
Ah, yes…
So, this is where we get to the main point, but...
On the day of the festival, Koyuba-san is scheduled to do a talk show and a mini live performance.
Yay! We get to hear Kaori-chan's singing!?
There aren't any particular problems with the mini live, and I think it'll be a wonderful stage if we leave it to the live staff.
The problem is the talk show.
We planned for her to talk about her memories here on Hitogashima, and promote the island's tourism-- That was the plan, but...
Was there some kind of problem?
Well, the thing is... It seems she hasn't decided what to talk about at all yet.
...That's right. When I was little, I spent a lot of time alone...
So my memories of this island are just about my favorite places with beautiful scenery, and I don't have anything that I can really talk about to people.
We think that's more than enough, though.
No... As content to talk about at a talk show, it's too weak.
Well, it's certainly true that like Koyuba-san says, if there was a story with more impact, we'd be more than happy to have it.
And that's where!! Shinpei-kun and Mio-chan come in!
Wh-What do you mean?
Could you show Koyuba-san around this island again?
In the process of doing that, she might be able to remember some interesting episodes she had forgotten until now.
If there's anything that could serve as a tourism appeal for this island, that's a bonus.
Is that something that only we can do?
That's right. Since you guys are young, you probably know what points would appeal to tourists nowadays, right?
I've been away from this island for about two years, though...
It'll be fine. This island hasn't changed at all in a mere two years.
More importantly, good for you, Shin-chan. You get to be partners with an idol.
Idiot! You've been nominated as a partner too!
Eh, really!?
Could you show her around some spots on this island that might appeal to young people, Mio-chan?
Show her around... Even if you ask me to do that.
I have to help out at the restaurant too...
Well, I'm sure you'll be busy right before the festival.
Are you two really okay with that...
Huh?
With what?
How do you think the number of tourists to Hitogashima has been doing over the past few years!
Even if you ask me that... I've never really thought about it.
It's plateauing... Or to be more accurate, it's slightly decreasing, right.
Oh, you actually read through the materials we sent you! I'm so happy.
It's for the sake of my beloved Hitogashima, after all!
The repeat rate isn't bad. The major problem is that we can't attract new <color=#GF_TIPS_090>tourists</color>.
At this rate, the island's tourism industry will only dwindle, and eventually die out completely! We have to make a new move!
So that's why this time... you brought in an active idol?
Exactly! This year's summer festival should see a huge influx of tourists coming just to see Koyuba-san!
We're aiming for a miraculous comeback event! We have to make Koyuba-san's live performance the key to reviving Hitogashima's tourism industry!
If we can just get them to come to this island, they should be able to understand how great this place is!
The repeat rate proves that, right!
The important thing is to get people to know about this island!
Yes! If we do that, I'm sure we can make them want to come visit this island!
What we need is that catalyst! And the only ones who can create it are you younger generations!
...Hah, hah.
What a passionate speech... What do we do, Shin-chan?
We can't really refuse after being talked to like that.
Kaori-chan seems to be getting pretty fired up too.
A-Ahaha... When I think about this island, I just end up putting a lot of energy into it.
Well, if we're good enough... Right, Mio?
Yeah, it seems kind of fun, too.
Ohhh! You two understand!
Well then, I'm counting on you! I'm counting on you too, Koyuba-san!
Yes, leave it to me!
Sorry, Ajiro-kun, Mio-chan. For getting you involved in something weird...
Don't worry about it. This kind of thing must be fate too.
Yeah. Anyway, let's try touring the island together.
Yeah...! Thank you, Ajiro-kun.
